subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV, OECD, WJP, RSF, TI) + Swiss official (FSO/BFS, SNB, SECO, Federal Council) + Swiss press (NZZ, Le Temps, SRF).
Watch-items: the post-Credit-Suisse UBS too-big-to-fail capital fight, and the 14-Jun-2026 SVP population-cap referendum that could threaten EU free movement.
Source-lean: hard data from non-Anglophone Swiss primaries; real-time political color (referendum polling, UBS) leaned Anglophone.

economic.notes: A top-tier fiscal/monetary anchor with a safe-haven currency; banking concentration and a near-deflation undershoot the watch-items.

fiscal_state.notes: Debt ~38% GDP with a constitutional debt brake and a 2024 surplus; AAA. (IMF Art IV 2025)

monetary_independence.notes: Own currency, a fully independent and highly credible SNB. (SNB)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The franc is a premier global safe haven (small reserve share); persistent appreciation pressure. (IMF COFER; SNB)

inflation.notes: Near zero, flirting with deflation (~0.3-0.5% forecast); price stability strong, the undershoot the concern. (FSO; SNB)

banking_stability.notes: Sound but extreme concentration post-Credit-Suisse (UBS balance sheet ~2x GDP); a 2026 Federal Council push for full capital backing of foreign units is contested - too-big-to-fail unresolved. (admin.ch; finews)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Pharma/precision/finance export strength with stable policy and no resource curse; a 2025 US tariff shock was cut to 15%, materially de-risking it. (SECO)

institutional.notes: Among the world's strongest institutions - direct democracy, independent courts and statistics, strong press freedom.

rule_of_law.notes: Global top tier (WJP ~94.6); direct democracy and an independent judiciary. (WJP 2025)

statistical_integrity.notes: The FSO is independent and fully standards-compliant. (FSO)

civil_service_capacity.notes: A high-capacity, rule-bound federalist administration; cantonal fragmentation a minor drag. (OECD)

press_freedom.notes: Strong (RSF ~89); watch-items on investigative-reporting restrictions and public-media funding. (RSF 2025)

political_social.notes: Exceptional stability and very strong rights; the immigration/EU free-movement debate the watch-item, and entry is hard for non-Europeans.

political_stability.notes: A consensus 'magic formula' Federal Council and direct democracy - exceptionally stable. (Swiss Federal Chancellery)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Very high quality of life, but hard for non-EU/EFTA (annual quotas/permits, slow naturalization ~10+ yrs); EU/EFTA enjoy free movement. (SEM; OECD)

social_cohesion.notes: High institutional trust and durable multilingual/cross-confessional cohesion. (FSO)

demographics.notes: Ageing, immigration-fed; a June-2026 '10-million' population-cap referendum (polling near-even) threatens EU free movement - a key watch-item. (FSO; SRG)

geopolitical.notes: A stable armed-neutral state with very low conflict exposure and an open wealth hub.

alliance_reliability.notes: Armed neutrality, non-NATO/non-EU but predictable and stable; an active neutrality-vs-sanctions debate. (FDFA)

conflict_involvement.notes: No direct conflict, very low exposure. (observed)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Adopted EU Russia sanctions, a fully open capital account, a global wealth hub. (SECO)

physical_practical.notes: World-class infrastructure, healthcare, and safety; the long-run drag is Alpine climate change, and healthcare is expensive.

climate.notes: Alpine glacier melt and permafrost/rockfall hazard, but highly adaptive and otherwise low extreme exposure. (FOEN/MeteoSwiss)

healthcare.notes: Excellent universal outcomes and access, but among the world's most expensive. (OECD; FOPH)

crime_safety.notes: Very low violent crime - a clear strength. (FSO/FEDPOL)

disaster_insurance.notes: Strong cantonal building-insurance systems and well-managed Alpine natural-hazard mapping. (FOEN)

summary: Among the world's strongest profiles: a top-tier debt-brake fiscal position, a fully independent SNB and a safe-haven franc, world-leading rule of law and direct democracy, exceptional political stability, and world-class infrastructure, healthcare, and safety. The drags are post-Credit-Suisse banking concentration (an unresolved too-big-to-fail question), hard immigration access for non-Europeans (with a live free-movement referendum), expensive healthcare, and long-run Alpine climate change.
